Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Lorentzen Co.
Off Calendar Nov-30-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Michael Espinosa, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Betty Fisher, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Melvin Fisher, Deceased, and Richard Fisher, Jesse Fisher and Michael Fisher, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Melvin Fisher, Deceased.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Lorentzen Co.
Default was entered against defendant on February 29, 2016.
Statement of Damages was filed on February 29, 2016.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on November 29, 2017.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Betty Fisher, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Melvin Fisher, Deceased, and Richard Fisher, Jesse Fisher and Michael Fisher, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Melvin Fisher, Deceased, and against the Defaulted Defendant Lorentzen Co., as follows:

Economic Damages - $216,522.00.
Non-Economic Damages - $14,000.000.00.
Total - $14,216,522.00.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
